title: nmatransport: Reproducibility Capsule for Transported Network Meta-Analysis
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: Can a reproducibility capsule turn an evidence-synthesis repository into a reviewer-auditable submission without restaging the workflow? We audited the shipped project using 2 source files, 2 test files, 3 manuscript or guide files, and 0 dashboard or figure assets committed locally. The capsule packages a micro-paper, a machine-readable config, an interactive reader, and a protocol so the repository can be inspected across reviewers. Across the inventory, the repository yields a documentation proportion of 0.43, with file-count range 0-3 across core surfaces, while exposing 2 entry points and 11 declared dependencies. Git metadata, file counts, and copied assets provide a stable local audit trail even when engine outputs remain outside the submission bundle. This packaging step converts a diffuse codebase into a citable, inspectable micro-publication suitable for rapid editorial triage and downstream peer review. The capsule does not verify scientific correctness itself; it standardizes what reviewers receive first, and deeper validation still depends on tests and manuscripts.

notes.data: Repository inventory with 2 source files, 2 test files, 3 documents, and 0 assets.

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a quantitative result such as a percentage, count, or interval.

checks.name: result sentence has estimand
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.

checks.name: boundary sentence present
checks.detail: Sentence 7 should express a limitation, harm, or scope boundary.

references: Salanti G. Indirect and mixed-treatment comparison, network, or multiple-treatments meta-analysis. Res Synth Methods. 2012;3(2):80-97.
Rucker G, Schwarzer G. Ranking treatments in frequentist network meta-analysis. BMC Med Res Methodol. 2015;15:58.
Dias S, Welton NJ, Caldwell DM, Ades AE. Checking consistency in mixed treatment comparison meta-analysis. Stat Med. 2010;29(7-8):932-944.
